infobox.value: "**Chuck**" **Flores** (January 5, 1935 – November 24, 2016) was an American [jazz drummer](/wiki/Jazz_drumming "Jazz drumming"). One of the relatively small number of musicians associated with [West Coast jazz](/wiki/West_Coast_jazz "West Coast jazz") who were actually from the West Coast, Flores was born in [Orange, California](/wiki/Orange,_California "Orange, California"), and grew up in [Santa Ana](/wiki/Santa_Ana,_California "Santa Ana, California"). He is best known for the work he did with saxophonist [Bud Shank](/wiki/Bud_Shank "Bud Shank") in the 1950s, and for his two-year stint with [Woody Herman](/wiki/Woody_Herman "Woody Herman"), from 1954 to 1955, but also performed and recorded with such musicians as [Carmen McRae](/wiki/Carmen_McRae "Carmen McRae"), [Art Pepper](/wiki/Art_Pepper "Art Pepper"), [Maynard Ferguson](/wiki/Maynard_Ferguson "Maynard Ferguson"), [Al Cohn](/wiki/Al_Cohn "Al Cohn"), and [Shelly Manne](/wiki/Shelly_Manne "Shelly Manne"), who had been his drum teacher. Manne and others considered Flores an underrated drummer.
* *[The Bud Shank Quartet](/wiki/The_Bud_Shank_Quartet "The Bud Shank Quartet")* (Pacific Jazz, 1956)

* [Toshiko Akiyoshi](/wiki/Toshiko_Akiyoshi "Toshiko Akiyoshi") Lew Tabackin Big Band, *[Long Yellow Road](/wiki/Long_Yellow_Road_(Toshiko_Akiyoshi_%E2%80%93_Lew_Tabackin_Big_Band_album) "Long Yellow Road (Toshiko Akiyoshi – Lew Tabackin Big Band album)")* (RCA 1975)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American jazz drummer (1935–2016)

**Charles Walter** "**Chuck**" **Flores** (January 5, 1935 – November 24, 2016) was an American [jazz drummer](/wiki/Jazz_drumming "Jazz drumming"). One of the relatively small number of musicians associated with [West Coast jazz](/wiki/West_Coast_jazz "West Coast jazz") who were actually from the West Coast, Flores was born in [Orange, California](/wiki/Orange,_California "Orange, California"), and grew up in [Santa Ana](/wiki/Santa_Ana,_California "Santa Ana, California"). He is best known for the work he did with saxophonist [Bud Shank](/wiki/Bud_Shank "Bud Shank") in the 1950s, and for his two-year stint with [Woody Herman](/wiki/Woody_Herman "Woody Herman"), from 1954 to 1955, but also performed and recorded with such musicians as [Carmen McRae](/wiki/Carmen_McRae "Carmen McRae"), [Art Pepper](/wiki/Art_Pepper "Art Pepper"), [Maynard Ferguson](/wiki/Maynard_Ferguson "Maynard Ferguson"), [Al Cohn](/wiki/Al_Cohn "Al Cohn"), and [Shelly Manne](/wiki/Shelly_Manne "Shelly Manne"), who had been his drum teacher. Manne and others considered Flores an underrated drummer.

In later years, Flores became a highly sought after and renowned educator whose students included [Danny Seraphine](/wiki/Danny_Seraphine "Danny Seraphine"), [Chad Wackerman](/wiki/Chad_Wackerman "Chad Wackerman"), John Wackerman, [Brooks Wackerman](/wiki/Brooks_Wackerman "Brooks Wackerman"), [Ray Mehlbaum](/wiki/Ray_Mehlbaum "Ray Mehlbaum"), [Pete Parada](/wiki/Pete_Parada "Pete Parada"), Jamie Wollam, Jose Ruiz, and Zack Stewart. Flores was a longtime faculty member at [Musicians Institute](/wiki/Musicians_Institute "Musicians Institute") in Los Angeles.

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chuck_Flores&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Discography")]

### As leader

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chuck_Flores&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: As leader")]

* *Flores Azules* (Dobre, 1976)
* *Drum Flower* (Concord Jazz, 1977)

### As sideman

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chuck_Flores&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: As sideman")]

**With [Laurindo Almeida](/wiki/Laurindo_Almeida "Laurindo Almeida")**

* *Latin Guitar* (Dobre, 1976)
* *New Directions* (Crystal Clear, 1979)
* *Virtuoso Guitar* (Crystal Clear, 1977)

**With [Woody Herman](/wiki/Woody_Herman "Woody Herman")**

* *The Woody Herman Band! Part 1* (Capitol, 1954)
* *The Woody Herman Band! Part 2* (Capitol, 1954)
* *The Woody Herman Band! Part 3* (Capitol, 1954)
* *The Woody Herman Band!* (Capitol, 1955)
* *Road Band!* (Capitol, 1955)
* *Blues Groove* (Capitol, 1956)
* *Jackpot!* (Capitol, 1956)
* *Hey! Heard the Herd?* (Verve, 1963)
* *The Third Herd Vol. 2* (Discovery, 1982)

**With [Art Pepper](/wiki/Art_Pepper "Art Pepper")**

* *[Modern Art](/wiki/Modern_Art_(Art_Pepper_album) "Modern Art (Art Pepper album)")* (Intro, 1957)
* *The Art of Pepper* (Omegatape, 1957)
* *The Art of Pepper Vol. II* (Omegatape, 1958)
* *Omega Alpha* (Blue Note, 1981)

**With [Bud Shank](/wiki/Bud_Shank "Bud Shank")**

* *[The Bud Shank Quartet](/wiki/The_Bud_Shank_Quartet "The Bud Shank Quartet")* (Pacific Jazz, 1956)
* *[Jazz at Cal-Tech](/wiki/Jazz_at_Cal-Tech "Jazz at Cal-Tech")* (Pacific Jazz, 1956)
* *[Bud Shank Quartet Featuring Claude Williamson](/wiki/Bud_Shank_Quartet_Featuring_Claude_Williamson "Bud Shank Quartet Featuring Claude Williamson")* (Pacific Jazz, 1957)
* *[Flute 'n Oboe](/wiki/Flute_%27n_Oboe "Flute 'n Oboe")* (Pacific Jazz, 1957)
* *The Swing's to TV* (World Pacific, 1958)
* *[Holiday in Brazil](/wiki/Holiday_in_Brazil "Holiday in Brazil")* (Vogue, 1959)
* *[Latin Contrasts](/wiki/Latin_Contrasts "Latin Contrasts")* (World Pacific, 1959)
* *[Slippery When Wet](/wiki/Slippery_When_Wet_(Bud_Shank_album) "Slippery When Wet (Bud Shank album)")* (World Pacific, 1959)
* *[Blowin' Country](/wiki/Blowin%27_Country "Blowin' Country")* (World Pacific, 1959)
* *[Bud Shank Plays Tenor](/wiki/Bud_Shank_Plays_Tenor "Bud Shank Plays Tenor")* (Record Society, 1962)
* *[Brasamba!](/wiki/Brasamba! "Brasamba!")* (Pacific Jazz, 1963)
* *Live at the Haig* (Choice, 1985)

**With others**

* [Toshiko Akiyoshi](/wiki/Toshiko_Akiyoshi "Toshiko Akiyoshi") Lew Tabackin Big Band, *[Long Yellow Road](/wiki/Long_Yellow_Road_(Toshiko_Akiyoshi_%E2%80%93_Lew_Tabackin_Big_Band_album) "Long Yellow Road (Toshiko Akiyoshi – Lew Tabackin Big Band album)")* (RCA 1975)
* [Conte Candoli](/wiki/Conte_Candoli "Conte Candoli"), Russ Freeman, Art Pepper, *[Mucho Calor](/wiki/Mucho_Calor "Mucho Calor")* (Andex, 1958)
* [Neshama Carlebach](/wiki/Neshama_Carlebach "Neshama Carlebach"), *Soul* (Sameach Music, 1996)
* [Al Cohn](/wiki/Al_Cohn "Al Cohn"), [Bill Perkins](/wiki/Bill_Perkins_(saxophonist) "Bill Perkins (saxophonist)"), [Richie Kamuca](/wiki/Richie_Kamuca "Richie Kamuca"), *Three of a Kind* (RCA 1956)
* [Al Cohn](/wiki/Al_Cohn "Al Cohn"), Bill Perkins, Richie Kamuca, *[The Brothers!](/wiki/The_Brothers! "The Brothers!")* (RCA Victor, 1956)
* [Dick Collins](/wiki/Dick_Collins "Dick Collins"), *Horn of Plenty* (RCA, 1955)
* Dick Collins, *King Richard the Swing Hearted* (RCA Victor, 1955)
* [Gil Fuller](/wiki/Gil_Fuller "Gil Fuller"), *[Night Flight](/wiki/Night_Flight_(Gil_Fuller_album) "Night Flight (Gil Fuller album)")* (Pacific Jazz, 1966)
* [Floyd Huddleston](/wiki/Floyd_Huddleston "Floyd Huddleston"), *Happy Birthday, Jesus* (Dobre, 1977)
* [Shelly Manne](/wiki/Shelly_Manne "Shelly Manne"), *The Manne We Love* (Eastworld, 1979)
* [Dodo Marmarosa](/wiki/Dodo_Marmarosa "Dodo Marmarosa") & [Lorraine Geller](/wiki/Lorraine_Geller "Lorraine Geller"), *West Coast Piano Touch* (Vantage, 1992)
* [Carmen McRae](/wiki/Carmen_McRae "Carmen McRae"), *[The Great American Songbook](/wiki/The_Great_American_Songbook_(Carmen_McRae_album) "The Great American Songbook (Carmen McRae album)")* (Atlantic, 1972)
* [Shorty Rogers](/wiki/Shorty_Rogers "Shorty Rogers"), *A Live Jam Session Recorded at the Rendezvous Ballroom* (Jam Session 1979)
* [Cy Touff](/wiki/Cy_Touff "Cy Touff"), *His Octet & Quintet* (Pacific Jazz, 1956)
* Cy Touff, *Keester Parade* (Pacific Jazz, 1962)
